role_summary: SNCC field organizer; in this corpus's embedding her testimony clusters with Charles McLaurin, Walter Tillow, Martha Prescod Norman Noonan, and Ekwueme Thelwell.
ai_reading: Todd's top semantic neighbors are Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 308), Walter Tillow (entry 124, count 281), Martha Prescod Norman Noonan (entry 83, count 192), Ekwueme Michael Thelwell (entry 34, count 162), and Gwendolyn Zoharah Simmons (entry 53, count 150). The cluster crosses SNCC's Mississippi voter-registration network (McLaurin), its Washington office (Thelwell), its Northern support fundraisers (Noonan, Robinson via her own cluster), and the medical/labor-organizing Tillow node. The high counts (peak 308) suggest a long, topically dense oral history. Her Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision position is +0.72, far toward Strategic Vision, joining the cluster of SNCC women whose testimony the embedding reads as strategic-framed (Avery +0.69, Henderson +0.51, Robinson -0.21 the only exception in this band).
biographical_paragraph: Lisa Anderson Todd's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 79 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
